title: Consolidate Cabinet into an app-independent system catalog
goal: Migrate Cabinet from mixed semantic, maintenance and runtime surfaces to one app-independent system catalog that owns stable ecosystem meaning, truth ownership, relations and entrypoints but no operational status.

acceptance.assertion: Exactly one maintained authority matrix identifies every source owner and non-authoritative projection.
Catalog data, validation and rendering work with the external Cabinet application stopped and are not shaped by Cabinet room manifests or runtime state.
The maintained catalog contains no duplicated task priority, runtime health, merge readiness, active execution or scheduler state.
A single registry-derived ecosystem view is canonical for generated mapping; other views are explicit specialized projections or retired.
Legacy rooms and duplicate prose are linked or archived without losing durable decisions.
CI detects competing ecosystem canons, manually duplicated authority fields and prohibited operational-status fields.

metadata.source_decision: Operator decision on 2026-07-10 to replace the external Cabinet-app foundation with an app-independent catalog.

remaining_uncertainty: one registered location only partially attributable
Cabinet human use not directly measured

metadata.outcome: Cabinet now has one app-independent, status-free system catalog core with a single authority matrix, one canonical generated map, hash-bound legacy archives and fail-closed validation. No runtime or service effect occurred.

validated_acceptance.single-authority-matrix: Repository-wide validation permits exactly one maintained authority matrix and rejects manual authority assignments elsewhere.
validated_acceptance.app-independent-catalog: Catalog data, validators and renderers pass in an isolated bundle without the external app, room manifests or runtime tree.
validated_acceptance.no-operational-status: Canonical nodes, relations, claims, policy and authority data reject task priority, runtime health, merge readiness, active execution, scheduler state and related operational fields.
validated_acceptance.single-generated-map: rendered/ecosystem-registry-map.mmd is the one canonical generated map; the previous hand-maintained map is explicitly non-authoritative and unmaintained.
validated_acceptance.legacy-navigation: Dynamic claims and the earlier role decision are preserved bit-exactly with SHA-256 bindings and linked through the archive index.
validated_acceptance.validator: CI rejects competing canons, duplicated authority assignments, prohibited operational fields, automatic legacy workflows and a bridge probe bypassing the isolated legacy adapter.

regression_and_correction.finding: The first PR head moved dynamic claims out of the canonical path while the pinned Bureau compatibility probe still required the historical literal path registry/ecosystem/claims.jsonl.
regression_and_correction.correction: A hash-bound, nonpersistent CI sandbox copies only archived dynamic claims to the historical path; canonical stable claims remain unchanged and are never exposed as task candidates. Pipefail now propagates probe failure.
